channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Context Management
channel.description: Latest Context Management news in Context Engineering

item.title: How Attention Sinks Emerge in Large Language Models: An Interpretability Perspective
item.description: arXiv:2603.06591v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often allocate disproportionate attention to specific tokens, a phenomenon commonly referred to as the attention sink. While such sinks are generally considered detrimental, prior studies have identified a notable exceptio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, arxiv, large language model, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Structure-Aware Set Transformers: Temporal and Variable-Type Attention Biases for Asynchronous Clinical Time Series
item.description: arXiv:2603.06605v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electronic health records (EHR) are irregular, asynchronous multivariate time series. As time-series foundation models increasingly tokenize events rather than discretizing time, the input layout becomes a key design choice. Grids expose time$\times$v...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, transformer, attention, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LegoNet: Memory Footprint Reduction Through Block Weight Clustering
item.description: arXiv:2603.06606v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As the need for neural network-based applications to become more accurate and powerful grows, so too does their size and memory footprint. With embedded devices, whose cache and RAM are limited, this growth hinders their ability to leverage state-of-t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, fine-tuning, memory, compression | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MultiGen: Level-Design for Editable Multiplayer Worlds in Diffusion Game Engines
item.description: arXiv:2603.06679v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Video world models have shown immense promise for interactive simulation and entertainment, but current systems still struggle with two important aspects of interactivity: user control over the environment for reproducible, editable experiences, and s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, memory, arxiv, context, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)
